connectorUiConfig.title: Elastic Agent (via Codeless Connector Framework)
connectorUiConfig.descriptionMarkdown: The Elastic Agent data connector enables you to ingest system metrics, logs, and telemetry data collected by Elastic Agent from Elasticsearch into Microsoft Sentinel. This connector uses the Elasticsearch Search API with API key authentication to query multiple data streams (CPU, memory, process, filesystem, network, load, uptime, agent metrics, and logs). It supports DCR-based ingestion time transformations for efficient query execution. For more information, see the API documentation: https://www.elastic.co/docs/api/doc/elasticsearch/operation/operation-search

sampleQueries.query:
ElasticAgentLogsV2_CL
| where DataStreamDataset == 'system.cpu'
| project TimeGenerated, HostHostname, SystemCpuTotalNormPct, SystemCpuUserNormPct, SystemCpuSystemNormPct, SystemCpuIdleNormPct
| order by TimeGenerated desc
| take 100

ElasticAgentLogsV2_CL
| where DataStreamDataset == 'system.memory'
| project TimeGenerated, HostHostname, SystemMemoryUsedPct, SystemMemoryActualUsedPct, SystemMemoryTotal
| order by TimeGenerated desc
| take 100

ElasticAgentLogsV2_CL
| where DataStreamDataset == 'system.filesystem'
| project TimeGenerated, HostHostname, SystemFilesystemMountPoint, SystemFilesystemUsedPct, SystemFilesystemFree, SystemFilesystemTotal
| order by SystemFilesystemUsedPct desc
| take 50

ElasticAgentLogsV2_CL
| where DataStreamDataset == 'system.network'
| project TimeGenerated, HostHostname, SystemNetworkName, SystemNetworkInBytes, SystemNetworkOutBytes, SystemNetworkInPackets, SystemNetworkOutPackets
| order by TimeGenerated desc
| take 50

ElasticAgentLogsV2_CL
| where isnotempty(MonitoringMetricsSystemLoad1)
| project TimeGenerated, HostHostname, MonitoringMetricsSystemLoad1, MonitoringMetricsSystemLoad5, MonitoringMetricsSystemLoad15
| order by TimeGenerated desc
| take 50

ElasticAgentLogsV2_CL
| where isnotempty(SystemProcessCpuTotalTicks)
| project TimeGenerated, HostHostname, ElasticAgentProcess, SystemProcessCpuTotalTicks, SystemProcessMemorySize
| order by SystemProcessCpuTotalTicks desc
| take 50

ElasticAgentLogsV2_CL
| where DataStreamType == 'logs'
| where isnotempty(LogLevel)
| summarize Count = count() by LogLevel
| order by Count desc

ElasticAgentLogsV2_CL
| where DataStreamType == 'logs' and LogLevel == 'error'
| project TimeGenerated, HostHostname, LogLevel, LogLogger
| order by TimeGenerated desc
| take 50

ElasticAgentLogsV2_CL
| summarize Count = count() by DataStreamDataset
| order by Count desc

ElasticAgentLogsV2_CL
| summarize Count = count(), LastSeen = max(TimeGenerated) by AgentName, AgentVersion, HostHostname
| order by LastSeen desc

dataTypes.name: ElasticAgentLogsV2_CL
dataTypes.lastDataReceivedQuery:
ElasticAgentLogsV2_CL
| where TimeGenerated > ago(10h)
| summarize Time = max(TimeGenerated)
| where isnotempty(Time)

resourceProvider.permissionsDisplayText: Read and Write permissions are required.

instructionSteps.title: 1. Prerequisites
instructionSteps.description: Ensure you have the required access and configuration.

parameters.content:
## Prerequisites

- An Elasticsearch deployment (self-managed or Elastic Cloud)
- Elastic Agent deployed with System integration enabled
- Agent monitoring enabled for logs and metrics
- Elasticsearch API key with read permissions on all indices
- Network connectivity from Microsoft Sentinel to your Elasticsearch endpoint

### Required Indices

The connector queries the following Elasticsearch indices:

**Metrics:**
- `metrics-system.cpu-*` - CPU metrics
- `metrics-system.memory-*` - Memory metrics
- `metrics-system.process-*` - Process metrics
- `metrics-system.filesystem-*` - Filesystem metrics
- `metrics-system.network-*` - Network metrics
- `metrics-system.load-*` - System load (Linux only)
- `metrics-system.uptime-*` - System uptime
- `metrics-elastic_agent.*` - Agent telemetry

**Logs:**
- `logs-elastic_agent-*` - Agent logs

instructionSteps.title: 2. Configure Elasticsearch Connections
instructionSteps.description: Add one or more Elasticsearch connections to collect data from.

parameters.content:
## Elasticsearch Connections

You can add multiple connections to collect data from different Elasticsearch deployments. Each connection requires its own Elasticsearch URL and API key.

### Creating an API Key

1. In Kibana, go to **Stack Management > API Keys**
2. Click **Create API key**
3. Set a name and configure permissions:
   - Read access to `metrics-system.*`
   - Read access to `metrics-elastic_agent.*`
   - Read access to `logs-elastic_agent-*`
4. Copy the Base64-encoded API key value

### Connection Details

Provide the connection details for your Elasticsearch deployment.

parameters.text: The connector name helps you identify this connection in the list. Use a descriptive name like 'Production Cluster' or 'US-East Environment'.

parameters.label: API Key
parameters.placeholder: Enter your Elasticsearch API key (base64 encoded)
parameters.name: apiKey

parameters.text: The API key will be securely stored. Use the Base64-encoded format from Kibana: `id:api_key`.
